label: National Rail – Normal Service – Single or Return

cancellation_windows.label: Early cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested 48 or more hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Standard cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 24 and 48 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Late cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 2 and 24 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested less than 2 hours before departure, or after departure time has passed

return_ticket_notes: For return tickets, each leg is assessed independently. If the outbound leg has already been travelled, only the return leg amount is eligible for refund, assessed against the return departure time.
no_show_policy: Passengers who do not travel and do not cancel in advance are treated as a no-show. No refund is issued for no-shows.

label: National Rail – Express Service – Single or Return

cancellation_windows.label: Early cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested 48 or more hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: Late cancellation
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested between 24 and 48 hours before scheduled departure

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested less than 24 hours before departure, or after departure time has passed

return_ticket_notes: Same as normal service: each leg assessed independently against its own departure time.
no_show_policy: No refund issued for no-shows on express services.

label: Metro – Single Ticket

cancellation_windows.label: Full refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested before the passenger has tapped in at the origin station
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'confirmed' and journey has not commenced

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Journey has already commenced (passenger has tapped in)
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'completed' or travelled_at is not null

notes: Metro single tickets are valid on the travel date only. A booking with status 'cancelled' is already cancelled and no further refund action is needed.

label: Metro – Day Pass

cancellation_windows.label: Full refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Cancellation requested before the passenger has made their first tap-in on the travel date
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'confirmed' and travelled_at is null

cancellation_windows.label: No refund
cancellation_windows.condition: Day pass has been activated (first tap-in recorded)
cancellation_windows.status_condition: Booking status is 'completed' or travelled_at is not null

notes: Day passes are non-transferable and valid only for the calendar day of purchase.

label: Delay Compensation – All Networks

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 30–59 minutes due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 50% refund of the fare for the delayed leg
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app or customer service within 28 days of travel date

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 60–119 minutes due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 100% refund of the fare for the delayed leg
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim via app or customer service within 28 days of travel date

compensation_rules.condition: Train delayed 120 minutes or more due to operator fault
compensation_rules.compensation: 100% refund of the fare for the delayed leg, plus reimbursement of alternative transport costs up to $10.00
compensation_rules.how_to_claim: Submit claim with receipts via customer service within 28 days of travel date

exclusions: Delays caused by third-party events (extreme weather, security incidents, government directives, or passenger-caused incidents) are not eligible for compensation.
operator_fault_definition: Signalling or dispatching issue within operator control
Staffing or operational error caused by the operator
Train or metro service removed from operation for operator-controlled maintenance reasons
non_operator_fault_examples: Passenger-caused incident or medical emergency on board
clarification: This exclusion applies only to delay compensation claims. Refund and rebooking eligibility for extreme weather or natural disaster events is handled separately under RF009.

label: Service Cancellation or Major Disruption – All Networks

eligibility.condition: Scheduled service is cancelled by the operator before departure
eligibility.notes: Passenger may choose either a full refund or rebooking onto the next available equivalent service at no extra charge, subject to seat availability.

eligibility.condition: Passenger cannot complete the journey due to operator-caused line suspension or station closure
eligibility.notes: Applies only when no reasonable alternative route is provided within 60 minutes.

eligibility.condition: Metro day pass purchased but no journey has been made before a full-day metro network suspension
eligibility.notes: If the day pass has already been used, normal delay compensation under RF005 may apply only when the disruption is due to operator fault; natural disaster cases are handled under RF009.

rebooking_conditions: Free rebooking applies only to the same route, same network, and equivalent fare class, subject to seat availability. Different routes, upgrades, or ticket-type changes require customer service approval.
relationship_to_RF009: If the cancellation or disruption is caused by extreme weather or a natural disaster, RF009 takes precedence over RF006. RF006 applies to operator-caused cancellations or disruptions that are not natural-disaster events.

label: Payment Error or Duplicate Charge – All Networks

eligibility.condition: Passenger was charged more than once for the same booking or metro trip
eligibility.notes: Only the duplicate or excess payment amount is refunded. The valid ticket remains active.

eligibility.condition: Payment was taken but no booking, ticket, or QR code was issued
eligibility.notes: Passenger may request either a full refund or manual ticket reissue after payment verification.

processing_time: Refunds are processed within 5 business days after verification. Actual posting time may vary depending on the passenger's bank or payment provider.

label: Seat Selection Fee Refund – National Rail

eligibility.condition: Passenger cancels a standard class booking and the fare is eligible for refund under RF001 or RF002
eligibility.refund: Seat selection fee is refunded in the same percentage as the fare refund.

eligibility.condition: Operator changes the passenger's selected seat for operational reasons
eligibility.refund: Seat selection fee is refunded in full.

exclusions: Seat selection fee is not refunded when the passenger voluntarily changes seat preference after booking.

label: Extreme Weather and Natural Disaster Disruption – All Networks

disruption_events.definition: Events outside operator control that may affect safe railway or metro operations.

eligibility_rules.condition: Service cancelled before departure due to natural disaster or severe weather
eligibility_rules.alternative_option: Passenger may choose a full refund or free rebooking within 7 days of the original travel date. Rebooking must be for an equivalent route and fare class unless customer service authorises an exception.

eligibility_rules.condition: Passenger chooses not to travel after an official government severe weather warning is issued
eligibility_rules.requirements: Applies only to journeys scheduled within the official warning period.

eligibility_rules.condition: Journey already commenced before service suspension
eligibility_rules.notes: Partial refund applies only to single or return tickets where the unused portion can be identified and no replacement transport is provided. Activated metro day passes are not eligible for partial refund unless customer service authorises an exception during a full-network emergency.

eligibility_rules.condition: Metro day pass purchased but metro network suspended before first use

compensation_policy.delay_compensation: Delay compensation under RF005 does not apply when delays or cancellations are caused by extreme weather or natural disasters outside operator control.
compensation_policy.alternative_transport: Taxi, rideshare, hotel, or accommodation expenses are not reimbursed unless explicitly approved by customer service during major emergencies.

passenger_guidance.service_updates: Passengers should check the mobile app, station announcements, or customer service channels for live disruption updates.
passenger_guidance.safety_priority: Service suspensions may occur without notice if operating conditions are unsafe.

rebooking_conditions: Free rebooking under RF009 is subject to seat availability and applies only to equivalent routes, equivalent fare classes, and the same passenger.
relationship_to_RF005: RF009 covers refunds and rebooking for extreme weather or natural disasters. RF005 delay compensation does not apply to these events unless the delay is separately confirmed as operator fault.
precedence: RF009 takes precedence over RF006 when the same cancellation or suspension is caused by extreme weather or natural disaster. RF005 delay compensation does not apply to RF009 events unless customer service explicitly authorises an exception.

label: Metro Online Single-Ticket Purchase
summary: Registered users may purchase metro single tickets and day passes through the TransitFlow mobile app for same-day travel. Payment is taken at purchase via credit card, debit card, or e-wallet. A digital QR ticket is issued immediately. Metro single tickets follow RF003 for cancellation before first tap-in. Day passes follow RF004 before first use. This policy supplements booking_rules.json metro online_single_ticket settings.
rules: Single tickets: priced using stops-based formula from metro_schedules.json (base_fare_usd + stops_travelled × per_stop_rate_usd).
Day passes: flat $5.00 for unlimited metro travel on the purchase date.
Tickets are valid only on the selected travel date; no advance purchase beyond the calendar day of travel.
notes: National rail online booking remains subject to national_rail rules in booking_rules.json.
